page: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The configuration provides 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread per core ratio, paired with a substantial 224.0 GB of system memory, yielding 28.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 459 GB, and there are no onboard GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates that the GS4-8 exhibits modest CPU performance, with single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 10% tier, and multi-core performance categorized as weak. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 454.13 GB/sec, large-block uncached write bandwidth is poor at 13.09 GB/sec. However, the system maintains average performance for database operations, Redis SET operations at 6.85 million operations per second, and small-model LLM inference text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the GS4-8 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-frequency CPU performance. The primary tradeoff is the older Intel Xeon v3 architecture, which lacks modern instruction sets like AVX-512 and exhibits weak single-threaded execution speeds.

This server is best suited for memory-bound applications, including medium-sized databases, caching layers such as Redis, and memory-intensive data processing tasks. It is less appropriate for compute-heavy workloads, compilation, or real-time rendering due to its lower CPU benchmarks.
description: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and a substantial 224.0 GB of RAM, translating to 28.0 GB of memory per core, alongside 459 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmark results show poor single-core and weak multi-core CPU performance, though it maintains average throughput for database operations and Redis SET actions. This hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff: high memory density paired with older, slower compute capabilities. Consequently, the GS4-8 is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and database operations that do not demand high-frequency CPU processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 224 GB of RAM, and 459 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and 459 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring 224 GB of RAM and local storage, optimized for caching and memory-bound database workloads.

High memory density of 224.0 GB RAM yielding 28.0 GB per core
Includes 459 GB of local storage with no GPU acceleration
Average performance in database and Redis SET operations
Best suited for memory-bound applications and caching layers
